title: When Interpreting Behavior: - Pause: Ask Yourself,
subtitle: Pause Before Assuming Hostility
description:
When interpreting behavior:
- Pause: Ask yourself, "Could this be unintentional or neutral?"
- Clarify: If unsure, communicate and seek their perspective.
- Reflect: Check if past experiences are influencing your assumptions.

Example: If someone doesn’t reply to an email, consider they might be busy, not ignoring you.

howTo.title: Pause and Reflect
howTo.summary: When faced with ambiguous behavior, take a 20 to 90-second pause. Ask yourself if the behavior could be unintentional or neutral, and consider alternative explanations.

howTo.title: Communicate for Clarity
howTo.summary: If uncertainty remains, send a neutral, clarifying message. Frame your question to invite perspective without assuming hostility, keeping emotions out of the initial communication.

howTo.title: Document Your Insights
howTo.summary: After each pause and communication, write a brief note in your journal. Record the situation, your neutral explanation, and the action taken to track your progress.

howTo.title: Tally Your Progress
howTo.summary: At the end of the day, count how many pauses you took and how many clarifying messages you sent. This helps you measure your improvement over time.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and refine your approach. Access it at https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/pause-before-assuming-hostility/.
